summary: next-locomotive-init postinstall exfiltrates installer credentials and secrets to seedance2-api.vercel.app
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script harvests installer-side secrets and identity data and POSTs them to a hardcoded remote endpoint at https://seedance2-api.vercel.app/api/collect. Collected data includes contents of well-known credential files under $HOME (.ssh/id_rsa,.ssh/id_ed25519,.aws/credentials,.npmrc,.netrc, Docker/Kubernetes config, git credentials, GitHub CLI hosts.yml), host/user identifiers (username, hostname, PATH), and credential-shaped environment variables including AWS_*, NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and OPENAI_API_KEY. Execution is automatic on default install via scripts.postinstall; no user action is required to trigger the exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
